goal: Land the Ruff (G7 Quality, P0 priority) external MCP plugin manifest with a real-subprocess `tools/list` health-check integration test as the THIRD test inside the existing `mod g7_plugin_manifests { ... }` block in tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs, mirroring the WO-0076 ESLint + Semgrep template scaled-down to one feature per the WO-0072/0077/0079 SOLO-feature shape. Reverses DEC-0020's premature deferral per DEC-0023 (the canonical Ruff MCP server is shipped as `mcp-server-analyzer` on PyPI by Anselmoo, combining Ruff + Vulture into one MCP server — not under the `mcp-ruff` / `ruff-mcp` / `ruff-mcp-server` names the prior planner triad searched).
plan_summary: Solo-feature G7 plugin-manifest landing WO mirroring WO-0079 (graphiti G3 solo-feature shape) verbatim, retargeted from G3-knowledge namespace to G7-quality namespace. Add plugins/quality/ruff/plugin.toml + scripts/devtools/install-ruff-mcp.sh + replacement scripts/verify/P3-W11-F03.sh + ONE new test `ruff_manifest_health_check` inserted into the existing `mod g7_plugin_manifests { ... }` block in crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs (between the existing `semgrep_manifest_health_check` at line 304 and the module's closing brace at line 425, additive only — DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out for the small ~70-LOC commit). Tier-1 install path: `uvx mcp-server-analyzer@<PINNED-VERSION>` per DEC-0023 §Decision point 2(Tier 1) (mirrors WO-0076 ESLint pattern of `npx -y @eslint/mcp@0.3.5` AND WO-0076 Semgrep pattern of `uvx semgrep-mcp@0.8.1` — uvx-managed venv self-resolves Ruff binary as a Python dep). Tier-2 fallback (per DEC-0023 §Decision point 2(Tier 2) + Decision point 4 — executor authority granted): if Tier-1 live capture fails (e.g., upstream `mcp-server-analyzer` does not advertise a clean `tools/list`, or the package is missing on PyPI), executor MAY vendor a ~50-line FastMCP wrapper at `plugin/ruff-mcp/` (NEW directory; granted under forbidden_paths exemption) shelling out to `ruff check --output-format=json` and `ruff format --diff` per the drewsonne/ruff-mcp-server source-precedent. Pre-baked M1/M2 mutation contract per the 7-WO mature pattern (WO-0066/0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0077/0079). F03 has `attempts=0` — first WO attempt; clean execution expected per the SEXTUPLE-verified G3→G4→G5→G6→G7→G8 paired-manifest template.
scope_in: 1. PRIMARY (Tier-1) install path per DEC-0023 §Decision point 2(Tier 1): `uvx mcp-server-analyzer@<PINNED-VERSION>` — mirroring the WO-0076 ESLint shape `npx -y @eslint/mcp@<PINNED-VERSION>` + Semgrep shape `uvx semgrep-mcp@<PINNED-VERSION>`. The `<PINNED-VERSION>` MUST be a frozen semver release tag captured at WO-execution time from `curl -sL https://pypi.org/pypi/mcp-server-analyzer/json | jq -r '.info.version'` (latest stable) OR pinned to a specific lower version if the latest exhibits a deprecation_notice surface (per WO-0076 Semgrep 0.9.0 → 0.8.1 pivot lesson §executor). The fallback `mcp-server-analyzer` (no version pin) is FORBIDDEN per AC15 + master-plan §13 + .claude/rules/commit-style.md (latest != stable canonical surface).

2. SECONDARY (Tier-2) fallback path per DEC-0023 §Decision point 2(Tier 2) + §Decision point 4 (executor authority granted to escalate to Tier-2 in the SAME WO if Tier-1 smoke fails — avoids an extra WO cycle): vendor a ~50-line FastMCP wrapper in NEW directory `plugin/ruff-mcp/` (granted under forbidden_paths exemption — UCIL has zero vendored MCP wrappers; this would be net-new infrastructure with the precedent set by drewsonne/ruff-mcp-server source repo). The Tier-2 wrapper exposes three tools mirroring drewsonne's source: `ruff_check` (shells out to `ruff check --output-format=json --no-cache`), `ruff_format` (shells out to `ruff format --diff`), `ruff_fix` (shells out to `ruff check --fix --output-format=json --no-cache`). Wrapper MUST use the official `mcp` Python SDK (`mcp[cli]>=1.0.0`) NOT a hand-rolled JSON-RPC implementation. Document the Tier-1-vs-Tier-2 decision tree in the manifest's leading comment block AND the RFR's §Tier decision section. Tier-2 selection MUST be triggered ONLY by a documented Tier-1 failure (live capture exit-code != 0 OR `tools/list` returning a deprecation_notice OR malformed JSON-RPC); Tier-1 SHOULD succeed first-attempt per the DEC-0023 §Rationale (Anselmoo's wrapper is the documented upstream of record).

3. Create plugins/quality/ruff/plugin.toml mirroring the structure of plugins/quality/eslint/plugin.toml AND plugins/quality/semgrep/plugin.toml (P3-W11-F02 + P3-W11-F04 / WO-0076): leading multi-line comment with `pinned <DATE>:` block (source URL = https://github.com/Anselmoo/mcp-server-analyzer for Tier-1 OR the vendored Tier-2 path; PyPI URL = https://pypi.org/project/mcp-server-analyzer/ for Tier-1; pinned tag, install command, advertised tools list with count, transport=stdio, operator-state requirements section [for Tier-1: NONE — uvx-managed venv self-resolves ruff per the upstream's Python deps; for Tier-2: ALSO NONE — wrapper bundles ruff as a Python dep]); `[plugin]` section (name='ruff', version=<pinned semver tag>, description citing master-plan §4.7 line 376 + §5.3 G7 source designation + 'Python lint + format via Rust per master-plan §4.7'); `[capabilities]` section with provides=['quality.ruff.lint', 'quality.ruff.format'] (or analogous quality.* names — namespace established by ESLint + Semgrep in WO-0076, these new capabilities EXTEND it cleanly per master-plan §5.3); languages=['python'] (Ruff is Python-language-keyed, contrast with ESLint=['javascript','typescript'] and Semgrep=[]); `[transport]` section with type='stdio', command='uvx', args=['mcp-server-analyzer@<PINNED-VERSION>'] for Tier-1 OR command='python' / args=['-m', 'plugin.ruff_mcp'] / cwd=<vendor-dir> for Tier-2 (executor selects path at WO execution time per Tier-1-vs-Tier-2 decision); `[lifecycle]` section mirroring F02/F04 exactly (hot_cold=true, idle_timeout_minutes=10).

4. The `transport.command` field MUST be the bare binary name `"uvx"` (Tier-1) OR `"python"` (Tier-2) — NOT an absolute path — per WO-0069 lessons §executor: the M1 transport.command-poison mutation pattern relies on swapping to `/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__`, AND absolute paths break across CI/dev environments.

5. Capture the live `tools/list` AT THE SPECIFIC PINNED VERSION before sealing the manifest's `[capabilities] provides` list and the test's expected-tool-name literal — per WO-0076 §planner lesson 2 + WO-0077 §planner lesson `serverInfo.name asymmetry`. Steps: (a) install via the chosen pinned ref (`uvx mcp-server-analyzer@<VERSION> --help` for Tier-1 warm-up — though `--help` may not be honored by the MCP server; that's fine, the uvx fetch primes the cache as a side-effect anyway, mirroring WO-0075 lesson §executor for filesystem-mcp), (b) drive the MCP `initialize` → `notifications/initialized` → `tools/list` handshake via a one-shot Python driver script (mirror the WO-0067/0069/0075/0076/0077 `/tmp/wo-NNNN-capture.py` shape), (c) capture the full advertised tool-list verbatim AND `serverInfo.name` (PyPI package name `mcp-server-analyzer` MAY differ from runtime `serverInfo.name` per WO-0077 lesson — document both verbatim in manifest top-of-file rustdoc), (d) embed the captured tool-list in the manifest's `[capabilities] provides` AND the test's SA-tagged panic literal. NB: If the v0.x.y release exhibits a single deprecation_notice tool (per WO-0076 Semgrep 0.9.0 → 0.8.1 pivot), executor MUST pivot down to the latest preceding release exposing the canonical tool surface AND document the pivot as a Disclosed Deviation in the manifest top-of-file rustdoc + RFR.

6. Operator-state-CLI-dependency classification per WO-0076/0077 §planner lesson `distinguish operator-state-CLI-dependent vs self-resolving MCP servers`: ruff is **SELF-RESOLVING** (uvx-managed venv handles ruff binary as a Python dep declared in `mcp-server-analyzer`'s pyproject.toml; for Tier-2, the vendored wrapper bundles ruff identically). Contrast with WO-0076's Semgrep MCP which required `semgrep --pro --version` lifespan call (operator-state-CLI-dependent); ruff has NO equivalent external CLI dep. Document the SELF-RESOLVING classification in scope_in's lifespan-handler section (manifest top-of-file rustdoc) and reflect in the verify script: NO `resolve_working_ruff()` helper, NO operator-state short-circuit on missing ruff binary. Single gate only: `if [[ "${UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E:-0}" != "0" ]]; then echo '[SKIP] P3-W11-F03: tool-level smoke skipped via UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E'; exit 0; fi` — the cargo-test `tools/list` path (Step 4 of the verify script) remains the load-bearing assertion regardless. NB: WO-0076 §executor lesson `unsafe std::env::set_var('SEMGREP_PATH', ...)` does NOT apply here — ruff needs no parent-process env-var mutation.

7. Extend crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs by ADDING a third test `ruff_manifest_health_check` INSIDE the existing `mod g7_plugin_manifests { ... }` block (between the closing brace of the existing `semgrep_manifest_health_check` at line ~370 and the module's closing `}` at line 425). Mirror the existing two tests' shape exactly: `#[tokio::test] async fn`, `if skip_via_env() { return; }`, load manifest via `PluginManifest::from_path`, manifest sanity asserts (name='ruff', transport.kind='stdio', !provides.is_empty()), `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout(&manifest, FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS)`, assert health.name='ruff' (or `serverInfo.name` if it differs), health.status=Ok, !health.tools.is_empty(), and a load-bearing assertion that one specific advertised tool name is present (executor selects the canonical Ruff MCP tool from the live capture — likely `ruff_check` / `analyze_python` / `lint_code` per the upstream's tool-list — and pins on it; rationale comment cites that pinning on the count would force updates on every benign upstream tool addition while pinning on a specific name surfaces upstream tool-surface drift loudly, mirroring the ESLint `lint-files` / Semgrep `semgrep_scan` rationale).

8. ADDITIVELY only — do NOT modify the existing two tests inside `mod g7_plugin_manifests { ... }` (eslint_manifest_health_check at line 233, semgrep_manifest_health_check at line 304). Confirm via `git diff main -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs | grep -E '^-' | grep -vE '^---' | wc -l` returning 0 (zero deletions; only added lines). Per WO-0069 lessons §executor: the existing module's two-test scope must not be mutated by additions. The leading rustdoc comment block at lines 1-73 may be amended ONLY to update the file-level overview (e.g., changing `ESLint + Semgrep G7` → `ESLint + Semgrep + Ruff G7`, listing all three test names in §Tests-wrapped paragraph at line 44); preserve all WO-0076/0075 carry-forward citations verbatim.

9. The new test lives INSIDE the existing `mod g7_plugin_manifests { ... }` block at module root (DEC-0007 frozen-selector module-root placement, carried per WO-0068 lessons §planner 'frozen-test selector substring-match REQUIRES module-root placement'). Confirm `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g7_plugin_manifests g7_plugin_manifests::ruff_manifest_health_check` resolves uniquely (substring-matches without requiring `--exact`).

10. Create scripts/devtools/install-ruff-mcp.sh mirroring scripts/devtools/install-eslint-mcp.sh + scripts/devtools/install-semgrep-mcp.sh verbatim in shape: `set -euo pipefail`, `PINNED_RUFF_MCP_VERSION=` constant, OK-when-uvx-on-PATH branch, MISSING branch with the exact pinned install command (`uvx mcp-server-analyzer@<VERSION> --help` for the Tier-1 warm-up; `--help` may not be honored by the MCP server — that's fine, the uvx fetch primes the cache as a side-effect anyway; for Tier-2, the script SHOULD instead `pip install -e plugin/ruff-mcp/` to install the vendored wrapper in editable mode and verify import via `python -c 'import plugin.ruff_mcp'`). Always `exit 0` (informational scaffolding — same contract as WO-0069/0072/0075/0076/0077/0079). Tier choice in the install script SHOULD match the manifest's chosen Tier (executor authors both at WO execution time after Tier-1 capture succeeds-or-fails).

11. Replace scripts/verify/P3-W11-F03.sh (currently TODO-stub returning exit 1) by copying scripts/verify/P3-W11-F02.sh OR scripts/verify/P3-W11-F04.sh verbatim and adapting: change pinned-version constant to the ruff-mcp commit-or-semver, change the cargo-test selector to `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g7_plugin_manifests g7_plugin_manifests::ruff_manifest_health_check`, and adapt the tool-level smoke section per scope_in #6 (single-gate pattern: only UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E check — no operator-state-CLI gate, since ruff is self-resolving). The smoke MUST exercise at least ONE end-to-end tool-call against the fixture python-project (e.g., `tools/call ruff_check {file_path: 'tests/fixtures/python-project/...py'}` returning at least one structured finding with `file/line/rule/severity` keys per F03 spec line: 'Python lint errors and format violations from the fixture python-project returned with file, line, rule, and fix suggestion.'). Print `[OK] P3-W11-F03` on green; `[FAIL] P3-W11-F03: <reason>` on red. Always `exit 0` on green / `exit 1` on red. The script remains read-only against tests/fixtures/** (forbidden_paths invariant) — fixture mutations (if needed for deliberate-lint-violation pattern per WO-0076 ESLint `bad.js` precedent) land in `mktemp -d` tmpdir copy ONLY.

12. Pre-baked mutation contract M1 (ruff transport.command poison): swap `command = "uvx"` → `command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"` in plugins/quality/ruff/plugin.toml; cargo test selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g7_plugin_manifests g7_plugin_manifests::ruff_manifest_health_check` must FAIL with `PluginError::Spawn { source: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound } }` (or the analogous spawn-error chain on the platform); restore via `git checkout -- plugins/quality/ruff/plugin.toml`; confirm md5sum matches the pre-mutation snapshot.

13. Pre-baked mutation contract M2 (expected-tool-name regression): in tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs's new `ruff_manifest_health_check` test, change the `t == "<canonical_ruff_tool>"` assertion to a non-existent tool name (e.g., `t == "NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M2"`); the test MUST FAIL with the structured panic carrying the full advertised-tool list (the `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` panic body itself doubles as a diagnostic dump of the live tool surface at the pinned version). Restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs`; confirm md5sum match.

14. Pre-flight word-ban grep MUST cover ONLY production-side files (manifest, install script, verify script, AND if Tier-2 is selected: `plugin/ruff-mcp/**` vendored wrapper) and EXEMPT integration-test files under `#[cfg(test)]`/`tests/`. The grep `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/quality/ruff/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-ruff-mcp.sh scripts/verify/P3-W11-F03.sh [plugin/ruff-mcp/**]` must return empty before first push. The integration-test file's rustdoc may mention 'Mocking ... is forbidden' inside a forbidden-pattern declaration (per WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0069 carve-out).

15. Pinned-immutable-ref policy: confirm `! grep -qE '"(main|latest|master|head|develop|dev|nightly)"' plugins/quality/ruff/plugin.toml` returns empty (the floating-ref class). Frozen semver release tags (e.g., `@1.2.3`) are acceptable; bare branch refs OR omitted version specifiers are forbidden. Per master-plan §13 + WO-0069 lesson §executor: floating refs make verifier reruns non-deterministic.

16. The integration-test file's `mod g7_plugin_manifests` block must use `tokio::process::Command` (not `std::process::Command`) for any helper subprocess invocations introduced by this WO — per WO-0075 §executor lesson W1 + WO-0076 pre-emptive carve-out. The existing `eslint_manifest_health_check` and `semgrep_manifest_health_check` use `tokio::process::Command` already; the new ruff test SHOULD follow the same minimalist shape. NB: F03's spec calls for 'Python lint errors and format violations from the fixture python-project returned with file, line, rule, and fix suggestion' — the verify script's tool-level smoke (NOT the cargo test) is the right place for the file-level lint-finding assertion. The cargo test's load-bearing assertion is the daemon-side spawn + tools/list handshake. Pre-flight `! grep -qE 'std::process::Command' crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs` (covers all three tests) before first push.

17. `unsafe std::env::set_var` IS FORBIDDEN in this WO's test code. Ruff does NOT need parent-process env-var mutation (contrast with WO-0076 Semgrep which used `unsafe set_var("SEMGREP_PATH", ...)` — that was the W1+W2 debt; this WO sidesteps it by virtue of ruff being self-resolving). NEW unsafe blocks must NOT be introduced by this WO. Per WO-0076 §verifier lesson 'unsafe block without ADR cite — soft critic-warning category'.

18. DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out applies to the augmentation of tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs (single small commit adding ONE test inside the existing `mod` block — likely <80 LOC). Cite DEC-0005 + WO-0079 commit `c448dfc` precedent (graphiti g3 augmentation) in the relevant commit body. Do NOT split the new test into multiple commits.

19. §15.2 tracing spans do NOT apply to plugin-manifest landings — these features add data files (TOML manifests) + integration tests + helper scripts, NOT new orchestration code. Do NOT introduce new `#[tracing::instrument(...)]` annotations under this WO. Cite WO-0067 lessons §planner #2 ('pure-deterministic / data-only modules: §15.2 tracing does NOT apply') as precedent.

20. WO-0069 lessons §planner #2 carve-out (pre-emptive API-key-gate for plugins requiring keys) does NOT apply here — ruff has NO API-key requirement (self-resolving Python lint, no LLM, no external service). The verify script's gate is single-axis: `UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E` only.

21. WO-0069 lessons §planner #3 carve-out (eventual-consistency OR-disjunction): ruff's tool-level smoke is fully synchronous — `ruff check --output-format=json` returns deterministic structured findings on first invocation. AND-conjunctive assertions are fine. No OR-disjunction needed (contrast with WO-0079 graphiti's eventual-consistency vector index).

22. AC25 reworded per WO-0070 lessons §planner #4: 'feat branch carries zero merge commits' (using `git log feat/WO-0080-ruff-quality-plugin-manifest ^main --merges` returning empty), NOT the 'feat..main = 0' form. Same spirit, more precise wording — tolerates 1-commit divergence between feat and main caused by gate-side critic-report commits between executor RFR push and verifier startup.

23. Carry-forward standing protocol (35 WOs deep, WO-0058..WO-0079): coverage-gate.sh sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER workaround — `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` is the authoritative measurement; the gate-script `coverage-gate.sh ucil-daemon 85 75` reporting `line=0%` is the known sccache interaction. Skip the `[FAIL] coverage gate: ucil-{core,daemon,embeddings}` lines in phase-3.sh output per scope_out + AC23 standing protocol.

24. Carry-forward standing protocol: AC30/AC31 phase-1/phase-2 effectiveness-gate flake (3 open escalations: 20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md, 20260507T1629Z-effectiveness-refactor-rename-python-fixture-missing-symbol.md, 20260507T1930Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-doctest-caller-flake.md). Executor runs constituent cargo-test + clippy + MCP-smoke + Serena-docker + diagnostics-bridge sub-checks in isolation; verifier re-runs the gate-script entry point from a clean session. Pre-existing standing scope_out item; awaiting dedicated harness-improvement WO.
25. Verifier MUST NOT set `UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS` OR `UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E` envs (per WO-0076 scope_in #12 + WO-0079 scope_in #25 precedent extended to this WO).

26. RFR (ready-for-review) MUST include the M1/M2 mutation contract section with: (a) named file path, (b) the exact patch (e.g., `s/command = "uvx"/command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"/`), (c) the targeted cargo-test selector, (d) the expected panic line, (e) the restore command (`git checkout -- <path>`), (f) the pre-mutation md5 snapshot path (`/tmp/wo-0080-<file>-orig.md5`). Mirror the WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0077/0079 RFR Mutation contract section verbatim in shape.

27. Pre-flight `which uvx` before first push — the integration tests will spawn from PATH, and missing `uvx` produces false-positive `PluginError::Spawn { kind: NotFound }` which masks real M1 detection. Per WO-0069 lessons §verifier #2 + WO-0076/0077/0079 carry.

28. Pre-baked md5 snapshots: take `md5sum plugins/quality/ruff/plugin.toml > /tmp/wo-0080-ruff-orig.md5` AND `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs > /tmp/wo-0080-g7tests-orig.md5` BEFORE applying any mutation. Confirm restoration via `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0080-<name>-orig.md5` returning OK after each `git checkout --` restore.

29. WO-0072 (codegraphcontext G4 solo-feature WO) + WO-0077 (mcp-pytest-runner G8 solo-feature WO) + WO-0079 (graphiti G3 solo-feature WO) are the canonical references for the SOLO-FEATURE plugin-manifest WO shape. Same shape applied here: ONE feature, ONE manifest, ONE install script, ONE replacement verify script, ONE additive test in an existing peer test file (g7_plugin_manifests.rs), ONE M1 + ONE M2 mutation contract. Avoid 2-feature WOs that would mix groups (G7 + something else).

30. F03 has `attempts=0` (DEC-0020 deferral was admin-only per the post-DEC-0022/0023/0024 lessons — no executor cycles consumed). This WO is the **first attempt**; clean execution expected per the SEXTUPLE-verified G3→G4→G5→G6→G7→G8 paired-manifest template. The blocking factor in DEC-0020 was 'no canonical PyPI/npm/GitHub repo' — that's now resolved by the `mcp-server-analyzer` PyPI install path discovered via broader PyPI search per DEC-0023 §Context. NO architectural ambiguities remain.

31. Apply WO-0076/0077 §executor lessons #1-#5 verbatim — they all carried forward. #1 (no kebab→snake translation; live capture documented in manifest comments — executor pins on the upstream's verbatim emission), #2 (no `--mcp` for warm-up; install script uses `uvx ... --help`), #3 (`UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E` documented in test rustdoc + verify script — extends existing G7 env-var, NO new env-var needed), #4 (python deadline polling in verify script: 90s init / 30s tools-list / 60s tools-call — ruff lints are fast, ~1-2s on the fixture), #5 (fixture copy BEFORE invoking — verify script uses file-by-file copy with `__pycache__` / `.pytest_cache` prune per WO-0077 §executor lesson if any pycache dirs exist in the fixture).

32. PyPI package name vs serverInfo.name asymmetry capture per WO-0077 §planner lesson `Capture BOTH PyPI/npm package name AND serverInfo.name`: PyPI package = `mcp-server-analyzer`; runtime `serverInfo.name` MAY differ (could be `mcp-server-analyzer` OR `ruff-vulture-mcp` OR `ruff-mcp` — executor's live capture is the source of truth). Document BOTH names verbatim in manifest top-of-file rustdoc when they differ. Manifest's `[plugin] name` field is `ruff` (the conceptual capability per UCIL's plugin namespace); the install string references the PyPI package; the test asserts on the runtime serverInfo.name.

33. Cold-cache uvx fetch budget per WO-0077 §executor lesson: `FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000` already set in tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs at line ~92 — covers the cold-cache uvx fetch (resolves mcp-server-analyzer + ruff + vulture + mcp Python SDK + transitive deps + downloads wheels) which takes up to ~30s; subsequent runs are <1s. NO change to FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS needed.

34. Ruff is SELF-RESOLVING per WO-0076/0077 §planner lesson `distinguish operator-state-CLI-dependent vs self-resolving`. NO `resolve_working_ruff()` helper, NO operator-state short-circuit, NO `RUFF_PATH` env var. The Tier-1 install path (mcp-server-analyzer on PyPI) bundles ruff as a transitive dep via uvx-managed venv (mcp-server-analyzer's pyproject.toml declares ruff as a Python package dep). The Tier-2 vendored wrapper (if executor falls back) declares ruff as a Python dep in its own pyproject.toml identically. The W1+W2 unsafe-set_var debt from WO-0076 dissolves naturally for this WO.

35. F03 spec acceptance per master-plan §4.7 + feature description: 'Python lint errors and format violations from the fixture python-project returned with file, line, rule, and fix suggestion.' The verify script's tool-level smoke MUST exercise this end-to-end: copy `tests/fixtures/python-project` into a `mktemp -d` tmpdir, fabricate a deliberate-lint-violation file (e.g., `bad.py` with `import os, sys` for `E401 multiple-imports`, an unused variable for `F841 unused-variable`, or trailing whitespace for `W291`) per WO-0076 ESLint `bad.js` fabrication precedent, invoke `tools/call ruff_check` (or the upstream's actual tool name from live capture) against the deliberate-violation file, assert at least one structured finding with `file/line/rule/severity` keys is returned. Fixture-pristineness invariant holds; mutations land in tmpdir copy ONLY.

36. The deliberate-lint-violation file fabrication MUST be in the tmpdir copy ONLY — DO NOT add lint-violation `bad.py` files to `tests/fixtures/python-project/` (per forbidden_paths invariant + WO-0076 §executor lesson on tmpdir-fabrication). Standalone `bad.py` content suggestion (executor confirms exact violations match Ruff's default rule set): `import os, sys
x = 1
unused = 42
  trailing_ws = 'foo'   
` — yields E401 + F841 + W291 violations under default `select = ['E', 'F', 'W']`.
scope_out: 1. Do NOT implement a `RuffG7Source` adapter for ruff (deferred to a follow-up WO that wires the daemon-side plugin runtime against the F05 G7 executor / F01 G7-quality-pipeline-entry, both currently dep-blocked on each other).
2. Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` (WO-0044 P2-W6-F05/F06 regression guard) — keep that suite isolated per WO-0069 lessons §executor #2.

3. Do NOT modify the existing two tests inside `mod g7_plugin_manifests { ... }` (eslint_manifest_health_check, semgrep_manifest_health_check) — only ADD the third ruff test. The leading file-level rustdoc may be amended ONLY to update the file overview (e.g., add ruff to the test list at line 44-46) per scope_in #8.
4. Do NOT add new G7 plugin manifests beyond Ruff under this WO (Sonarqube, Snyk, Trivy, TruffleHog, Gitleaks, RuboCop, Biome are all future-WO scope per master-plan §14.1 + DEC-0023 §Consequences).

5. Do NOT wire the new manifest into any `PluginManager::initialize_all` / startup-discovery path beyond what already exists — the WO-0044 + WO-0069 + WO-0076 manifests are loaded on-demand by the integration tests via `PluginManifest::from_path(&manifest_path)`, and the new manifest should be loadable the same way without daemon-startup changes.
6. Do NOT introduce or modify any LLM-provider or embedding-provider configuration (ruff has no LLM dependency).
7. Do NOT introduce graph-DB or external-service sidecar provisioning — ruff is pure-Python lint, NO services beyond the uvx-managed venv.
8. Do NOT introduce any cross-WO dependency wiring (downstream WOs consume F03; that's their scope).
9. Do NOT add any new `tracing::instrument` annotations to source code (per scope_in #19).
10. Do NOT add or modify entries in `ucil-build/feature-list.json` — verifier flips `passes` via `scripts/flip-feature.sh`.

11. Do NOT modify any file under `tests/fixtures/**` — verify scripts are read-only against the fixture (per WO-0069 forbidden_paths). The deliberate-lint-violation `bad.py` fabrication lands in the `mktemp -d` tmpdir copy ONLY (per scope_in #35/#36).

13. Do NOT prematurely select Tier-2 (vendored wrapper) without first attempting Tier-1 (uvx mcp-server-analyzer). DEC-0023 §Decision point 4 grants Tier-2 fallback authority IN THE SAME WO only if Tier-1 fails — Tier-1 is the default per DEC-0023 §Rationale (Anselmoo's wrapper is the documented upstream of record; PyPI URL https://pypi.org/project/mcp-server-analyzer/ is reachable at planner time).
14. Do NOT introduce a new `UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_*` env-var — extend the existing `UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E` from WO-0076 verbatim (single env-var per group; new tests reuse the existing gate).
15. Do NOT introduce `unsafe std::env::set_var` blocks (WO-0076 W1+W2 debt). Ruff is self-resolving — no parent-process env-var mutation needed (per scope_in #17 + #34).
16. Do NOT modify scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh OR scripts/verify/effectiveness-gate.sh — the standing carry-forwards (35 WOs deep) remain out-of-scope.

17. Do NOT split the `plugin/ruff-mcp/` Tier-2 vendored wrapper across multiple commits if Tier-2 is selected — the ~50-LOC wrapper + pyproject.toml is a single coherent unit per DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out.

18. Do NOT introduce ESLint-style `eslint.config.js` config fabrication for ruff — ruff's default rule selection (`select = ['E', 'F']` per ruff's defaults) suffices to surface the deliberate-violation file's findings WITHOUT a per-tmpdir `pyproject.toml [tool.ruff]` block. If executor finds the upstream MCP server requires a `pyproject.toml` to enable ruff, fabricate it identically to WO-0076 ESLint pattern (tmpdir copy ONLY) — but prefer the no-config path per DEC-0023 §Rationale (Tier-2 wrapper is intentionally minimal).
acceptance: 1. plugins/quality/ruff/plugin.toml exists with `name = 'ruff'`, `transport.command = 'uvx'` (Tier-1) OR `transport.command = 'python'` (Tier-2), `transport.args` containing the PyPI install ref `mcp-server-analyzer@<PINNED-VERSION>` (Tier-1) OR the vendored wrapper module path (Tier-2), `[capabilities] provides` containing at least one `quality.ruff.*` capability, `[lifecycle] hot_cold = true`, and `[lifecycle] idle_timeout_minutes = 10`.

2. scripts/devtools/install-ruff-mcp.sh exists, is executable (chmod +x), shells out to `uvx mcp-server-analyzer@<VERSION> --help` (Tier-1) OR `pip install -e plugin/ruff-mcp/` + import probe (Tier-2), and exits 0 unconditionally (informational-only contract).

3. scripts/verify/P3-W11-F03.sh replaces the TODO-stub, exercises the cargo-test selector + the live `tools/call <ruff_tool>` smoke against the fixture python-project (with deliberate-violation file fabrication in tmpdir per scope_in #35/#36), and exits 0 on success / 1 on failure. Single-gate `UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E` short-circuit per scope_in #6.

4. crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs gains ONE new test `ruff_manifest_health_check` inside the existing `mod g7_plugin_manifests { ... }` block (between `semgrep_manifest_health_check` at line ~304 and the module's closing `}` at line 425); the test mirrors the existing two tests' shape and asserts on a load-bearing canonical ruff tool name from the live capture.

5. cargo test selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g7_plugin_manifests g7_plugin_manifests::ruff_manifest_health_check` resolves uniquely (substring-match without --exact), green from a `cargo clean` baseline.
6. `cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon -- -D warnings` green; `cargo fmt --check -p ucil-daemon` green.
7. M1 mutation (transport.command poison) FAILS the cargo-test selector with `PluginError::Spawn { source: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound } }`.
8. M2 mutation (expected-tool-name regression) FAILS the cargo-test selector with the SA-tagged structured panic carrying the full live `tools/list` array.
9. Both M1 and M2 mutations are reversible via `git checkout -- <file>`; md5sum match restored after each.

10. RFR file ucil-build/work-orders/0080-ready-for-review.md exists with: (a) summary, (b) M1/M2 mutation contracts in the canonical shape, (c) live `tools/list` capture transcript, (d) Tier-1-vs-Tier-2 decision narrative, (e) PyPI package name vs `serverInfo.name` documentation per WO-0077 §planner lesson.
11. Feat branch carries zero merge commits: `git log feat/WO-0080-ruff-quality-plugin-manifest ^main --merges` returns empty (per scope_in #22 + WO-0070 §planner #4).
